metadata.name: Crew — Smuggler's Copter crewed by Llanowar Elves becomes artifact creature, attacks
metadata.description: P1 controls Smuggler's Copter (a non-creature artifact Vehicle, 3/3 printed P/T, Crew 1, Flying) and Llanowar Elves (1/1). P1 activates the Crew 1 ability by tapping Llanowar Elves (power 1 >= Crew cost 1). The crew ability goes on the stack. Both players pass priority. The ability resolves: Smuggler's Copter becomes an artifact creature until end of turn (CR 702.122a, Layer 4 type change). After resolution, assert: Copter has Creature card type, Elves is tapped, Copter is 3/3. P1 then advances to the DeclareAttackers step and attacks p2 with the now-crewed Copter (Flying — p2 has no creatures to block). P2 takes 3 combat damage; p2 life: 40 → 37.
metadata.generation_notes: Smuggler's Copter oracle text (Scryfall verified): 'Flying. Whenever Smuggler's Copter attacks or blocks, you may draw a card. If you do, discard a card. Crew 1 (Tap any number of creatures you control with total power 1 or greater: This Vehicle becomes an artifact creature until end of turn.)' Type: Artifact — Vehicle. P/T: 3/3. Mana cost: {2}. IMPORTANT: Smuggler's Copter is NOT in the engine Available Cards list and has no CardDefinition in definitions.rs. This script requires a CardDefinition for Smuggler's Copter before harness validation can pass. The loot trigger (draw/discard when attacks or blocks) is deferred from the card definition (needs 'when attacks or blocks' trigger condition implementation). CR 702.122a: 'Crew N means Tap any number of other untapped creatures you control with total power N or greater: This permanent becomes an artifact creature until end of turn.' The crew action uses action='crew_vehicle', card='Smuggler's Copter', convoke=['Llanowar Elves'] (the harness reuses the convoke field for crew creature names per replay_harness.rs:406-407 comment). Llanowar Elves (power 1) meets the Crew 1 threshold. The ability is placed on the stack (CR 602.2: activated abilities use the stack). After both players pass, it resolves adding CardType::Creature to Smuggler's Copter via Layer 4 AddCardTypes continuous effect (UntilEndOfTurn). The Vehicle's printed P/T 3/3 becomes active once it is a creature (CR 301.7b: 'If a Vehicle becomes a creature, it immediately has its printed power and toughness.'). The engine stores power: Some(3), toughness: Some(3) on the object from the card definition; these become relevant when Creature type is active. After crewing: Llanowar Elves is tapped (cost paid at activation time per CR 702.122b), Copter is an artifact creature. Turn_number 4 is used so Smuggler's Copter has no summoning sickness (placed in initial state via builder which sets has_summoning_sickness: false). Note: summoning sickness DOES apply to Vehicles once crewed (ruling). For the initial_state we use turn_number 4 and place the Copter already on the battlefield (pre-existing, no summoning sickness). Initial phase is precombat_main to allow crewing before combat. Priority passes advance to DeclareAttackers. Copter has Flying — p2 has no creatures, so it is unblocked. P2 takes 3 damage (CR 510.1b). No SBAs from combat (p2 has 37 life, Copter took no damage). The beginning_of_combat step is skipped in the script (no actions needed there) — the script starts in precombat_main and the crew happens there, then priority_round advances through beginning_of_combat to declare_attackers automatically.

disputes.description: Smuggler's Copter is not in the engine Available Cards list and has no CardDefinition in crates/engine/src/cards/definitions.rs. This script will fail harness validation until Smuggler's Copter is authored with CardType::Artifact, subtype Vehicle, Crew(1) keyword, Flying keyword, power: Some(3), toughness: Some(3). Without the CardDefinition, the engine cannot enrich the initial-state object with the Crew keyword, the crew_vehicle command will fail to find KeywordAbility::Crew on the object, and the crew ability will not resolve. This is a step-5 gap from ability-wip.md (card definition pending).

script.step_note: P1 activates Smuggler's Copter's Crew 1 ability by tapping Llanowar Elves (power 1 >= 1). Crew ability goes on the stack. CR 702.122a.

actions.description: Initial: Smuggler's Copter and Llanowar Elves untapped on p1 battlefield. Copter is a non-creature artifact. Stack is empty. Life totals 40/40.
actions.note: Smuggler's Copter begins as a non-creature artifact (Artifact — Vehicle, no Creature type). Llanowar Elves is untapped with power 1, satisfying Crew 1 cost. No summoning sickness issues: both permanents were placed in initial state at turn 4 (builder sets has_summoning_sickness: false for initial-state permanents). Crewing does NOT require a creature to be free of summoning sickness (ruling: any untapped creature you control can be tapped to pay crew cost).

CR 702.122a: P1 activates Crew 1 ability of Smuggler's Copter. Taps Llanowar Elves (power 1 >= Crew cost 1) as the crew cost. CR 702.122b: Llanowar Elves 'crews a Vehicle' when tapped to pay this cost. The crew ability is placed onto the stack (CR 602.2: activated abilities use the stack). Priority resets to active player p1 (CR 116.3).

actions.description: After crew activation: crew ability on stack (count 1), Llanowar Elves is tapped (cost paid at activation), Copter still untapped on battlefield.
actions.note: CR 702.122a: Tapping the crew creatures is a cost paid at activation time, before the ability is placed on the stack. Llanowar Elves is now tapped. Smuggler's Copter itself is not tapped by the crew cost — the crew ability's effect (becoming a creature) has not resolved yet. The crew ability sits on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: P1 holds priority after activation; p1 passes, then p2 passes. Stack resolves top object (crew ability). CR 405.5: LIFO — crew ability is the only stack object.

resolution.note: CR 702.122a: The crew ability resolves — Smuggler's Copter becomes an artifact creature until end of turn. This is a Layer 4 (type-changing) continuous effect: AddCardTypes({Creature}), duration UntilEndOfTurn. CR 301.7b: Smuggler's Copter immediately has its printed power and toughness (3/3) now that it is a creature.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Activated ability resolves. Smuggler's Copter gains Creature card type until end of turn. No ETB trigger fires (the Copter was already on the battlefield — becoming a creature is not entering the battlefield; ruling: 'When a Vehicle becomes a creature, that does not count as having a creature enter the battlefield.').

actions.description: After crew resolves: Smuggler's Copter is now an artifact creature (3/3), Llanowar Elves remains tapped, stack is empty.
actions.note: CR 702.122a + CR 301.7b: Smuggler's Copter is now an artifact creature with power 3 and toughness 3. Llanowar Elves remains on the battlefield in a tapped state (it was tapped as the crew cost). The crew ability resolved — stack is empty. P1 now has an artifact creature with Flying that can attack.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack → advance from PreCombatMain through BeginningOfCombat to DeclareAttackers step. P1 receives priority in DeclareAttackers.

script.step_note: P1 declares Smuggler's Copter (now an artifact creature with Flying, 3/3) as an attacker targeting p2. P2 has no creatures to block. Copter is unblocked.

actions.note: CR 508.1: P1 declares Smuggler's Copter as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1a: Smuggler's Copter is untapped and free of summoning sickness (it was placed in initial state at turn 4 — builder sets has_summoning_sickness: false). CR 508.1f: Smuggler's Copter becomes tapped as a side effect of attacking. CR 508.1k: Smuggler's Copter becomes an attacking creature.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Smuggler's Copter is tapped (attacking), stack empty.
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Smuggler's Copter is tapped as part of the attack declaration. P1 holds priority. Note: Smuggler's Copter's loot trigger ('whenever attacks or blocks, you may draw a card...') is deferred from the card definition implementation.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack → advance to DeclareBlockers step.

script.step_note: P2 declares no blockers (controls no creatures). Smuggler's Copter (Flying) also could not be blocked by non-flying/non-reach creatures. Copter becomes unblocked.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P2 declares no blockers (no creatures to block with). CR 509.1h: Smuggler's Copter has no creatures declared as blockers → it becomes an unblocked attacker. CR 702.9a: Flying — Copter can only be blocked by creatures with Flying or Reach; p2 has neither.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority → advance to CombatDamage step. Engine executes combat damage as a turn-based action on entering CombatDamage.

script.step_note: CombatDamage step: Smuggler's Copter (power 3, unblocked) assigns 3 combat damage to p2 (CR 510.1b). P2 life: 40 → 37. SBAs: p2 has 37 life (no SBA 704.5a). Copter took no damage (no SBA 704.5g).

actions.note: CR 704.5a: p2 has 37 life (above 0) — no player-death SBA. CR 704.5g: Smuggler's Copter (3/3 artifact creature) took no damage — no lethal-damage SBA. No SBAs apply.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after CombatDamage resolves. Stack is empty → advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: Final state: p2 at 37 life (took 3 damage from unblocked Copter), Smuggler's Copter still on p1 battlefield (tapped from attacking), Llanowar Elves on battlefield (tapped from crew cost).
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Unblocked Smuggler's Copter (power 3) dealt 3 combat damage to p2. P2 life: 40 → 37. Smuggler's Copter remains on battlefield as an artifact creature (the UntilEndOfTurn continuous effect persists until cleanup step). Llanowar Elves remains on battlefield, tapped from paying the crew cost.
